text:
Subject:[External] [CRITICAL ALERT: ACT NOW] - Multiple vulnerabilities affecting NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway devices
 	 

Critical Alert - Act Now
27 August 2025

Dear ASD's ACSC Alert Service subscriber,

This alert has been written for technical IT services supporting organisations, such as critical infrastructure, and government. 

Background / What’s happened?

ASD’s ACSC is aware of multiple vulnerabilities impacting NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) products: 

CVE-2025-7775 (Critical) involves a memory overflow vulnerability leading to Remote Code Execution and/or Denial of Service.
CVE-2025-7776 (High) involves a memory overflow vulnerability leading to unpredictable or erroneous behaviour and Denial of Service.
CVE-2025-8424 (High) involves improper access control on the NetScaler Management Interface.
The following versions of NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway are affected:

NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway 14.1 BEFORE 14.1-47.48
NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway 13.1 BEFORE 13.1-59.22
NetScaler ADC 13.1-FIPS and NDcPP BEFORE 13.1-37.241-FIPS and NDcPP
NetScaler ADC 12.1-FIPS and NDcPP BEFORE 12.1-55.330-FIPS and NDcPP
Citrix reports active exploitation of these vulnerabilities has been observed.

Mitigation / How do I stay secure?

Australian organisations should review their networks for use of vulnerable instances of NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway. 


Updated software versions are available for affected NetScaler products.

Citrix’s Security Bulletin should be consulted for advice on detecting and updating vulnerable instances of these products. 

Additional details are also available at Critical security update announced for NetScaler Gateway and NetScaler

Assistance / Where can I go for help?

 
Organisations that have been impacted, suspect impact or require advice and assistance can contact us via 1300 CYBER1 (1300 292 371).

Read this alert on the website: Multiple vulnerabilities affecting NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway devices | Cyber.gov.au


Are you a victim of cybercrime? Visit ReportCyber to take your next steps.

We use hyperlinks to give you more information. If you don't want to click hyperlinks, you can search for the information on ASD's ACSC Website.

text:
Subject:	[External] Threat Alert: Recommended action to protect against Microsoft 365 Direct Send attacks
Hi Visweswaran,

As part of Barracuda’s ongoing commitment to protecting businesses, we want to alert you to a growing threat that we have recently identified: Direct Send attacks.

This threat arises from Microsoft 365’s Direct Send feature, which allows internal email delivery without requiring authentication. Barracuda has observed threat actors abusing this to bypass email security gateways and deliver internal-looking phishing emails from organizations’ own domains.

Microsoft's Direct Send capability is enabled by default – this is not a limitation of Barracuda offerings. To ensure you and your customers remain fully protected through Barracuda Email Gateway Defense, it is critical that you review and update configuration settings. 

Please follow these instructions. We strongly recommend taking immediate action to remediate this issue and reduce the risk of compromise.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact Barracuda Support.
Regards,

The Barracuda Networks Team
Contact: support@barracuda.com 
Phone:+1 408 342 5300

Subject: Implementation of SSPR and MFA for All MBIP Accounts
Date: 29 May 2025

Body:
Dear Team,

To strengthen the security of our MBIP accounts and protect our organizational data, we will be implementing Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users. We’ve successfully implemented the steps with Courtney, and everything worked as expected.

Steps to complete the setup:

Go to the link: aka.ms/ssprsetup and ensure you are logged into your company account.

Click “Add sign-in method” and select Microsoft Authenticator App.

Download the Microsoft Authenticator app from the App Store or Google Play.

Open the app, select Add Account → Work or School Account.

Scan the QR code displayed on your screen.

Once setup is complete, click Next and perform the test verification.

After successful verification, click Done.

Again click “Add sign-in method” and select Phone.

Enter your mobile number, choose to receive a text or call, and verify with the code received.

Once completed, reply to this email (not Reply All) with confirmation.

For assistance, contact help@jypragroup.com.au
 or call 1300 395 845.

Best regards,
Visweswaran Soundararajan
Senior Security Analyst, Jypra Group

Barracuda Logo
 
 
Partner Pulse
 
The latest news from Barracuda

August 2025
 
 
Annual Partner Success Program compliance review coming soon

The annual compliance review cycle starts September 1, 2025. During the review, partners will be assessed on their performance from September 1, 2024, to August 31, 2025, against the revenue and certification requirements for their designated Barracuda Partner Success Program level.

For a detailed look at your revenue and certification compliance to date, please check the Performance Dashboard in the Partner Portal. If you have any questions about your current program compliance status or your path to compliance, contact your Barracuda channel manager or email partnerprograms@barracuda.com. You can find the program requirements for your specific partner level in the Barracuda Partner Success Program Guide.
 
 
 
 
 
Recent uptick of Direct Send attacks
Date  |  Time
 
Barracuda blog: MSFT Direct Send phishing attacks explained
 
Threat actors are actively abusing Microsoft 365’s Direct Send feature to deliver phishing emails that appear to originate from internal users.

Microsoft 365’s Direct Send capability is enabled by default – this is not a limitation of Barracuda. However, to ensure our partners and customers are protected, we have updated Email Gateway Defense detection rules and notified administrators whose environments may be vulnerable. See our blog about this threat and how you can reduce risk for yourself and your customers.
 
Find out more
 
 
 
 
 
Inside July’s Email Threat Radar: Top attacks to know about
Date  |  Time
 
Barracuda Threat Radar - Jul 2025
 
Last month, Barracuda threat analysts uncovered a wave of sophisticated email-based threats targeting organizations worldwide, many powered by phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) kits. From fake toll violation scams, credential phishing attacks using Tycoon and LogoKit, and campaigns spoofing trusted brands like Autodesk, RingCentral, and SharePoint. Read on to find out how they happened and what you can do to stay protected now.
 
Read it here
 
 
 
 
 
How attackers poison AI tools and defenses
Date  |  Time
 
Barracuda Threat Spotlight: Poison AI tools
 
Did you know that generative AI is being used to create and distribute spam emails and craft highly persuasive phishing attacks? These threats continue to evolve and escalate - but they are not the only ways in which attackers leverage AI. Read on to learn how cybercriminals are advancing their use of AI to spam, scam and spoof victims, plus find out how email defenses need to adapt, and what you can do to stay ahead of the curve now.
 
Read it here
 
 
 
 
 
Beyond blocking: Email security needs a response plan
Date  |  Time
 
Barracuda on ITVoice
 	
As phishing, malware, and social engineering tactics evolve, attackers continually find new ways to slip past security, so having a strong response plan has never been more essential for catching what prevention misses. Click the link to hear from Pranay Manek, System Engineer Manager, Barracuda, India on everything you need to know when it comes to combining prevention with fast and scalable incident response.
 
Read it here
 
 
 
 
 
From defense to direction: the modern CISO playbook
Date  |  Time
 
Barracuda on Frontier Enterprise
 	
Boards no longer see CISOs as just defenders. They expect them to shape business decisions, guide growth strategies, and act as trusted risk advisors. But outdated playbooks of quarterly reviews and static controls can’t keep pace with SaaS-driven attack surfaces, shadow IT, and tool sprawl. text:
Subject:	[External] CanIPhish Newsletter: 10 New Training Modules Go Live!
 	 

August 2025 - Newsletter

We’ve packed a lot into August! We launched 10 new training modules, went live with our website redesign, and overhauled one of our most used features: phish reporting. Read on to find out more! 
🧠 10x New Training Modules: Our latest batch of training modules is here. Each one covers a high-impact security topic, designed to keep your employees sharp and your organisation secure. Check them out here!

new training modules banner image
🪝 Report Phishing Enhancements: We’re excited to announce that CanIPhish’s Report Email capability has been completely revamped and upgraded. Your employees will now have the ability to report both spam and phishing emails, and you as the administrator now have greater control over what notifications get sent out.

Additionally, we now support email quarantining! It’s an additional integration step, but by setting up email quarantining, you can control if emails are quarantined immediately after they’re reported, if they’re quarantined once positively attributed as a phish or spam, or even if they’re restored once identified as a false positive.

Alongside this, we’ve included an optional integration that allows you to report these emails to Microsoft or Google to help prevent similar emails from coming through in the future.

🖥️ Website Redesign Now Live: The CanIPhish front-end website just got a major refresh. It’s faster, cleaner, and better at showcasing what the platform can do. Whether you’re pointing stakeholders to key features or just browsing for resources, everything’s easier to find and easier on the eyes.​

🆕 New Posters & Cybersecurity Wallpapers: We’ve released a fresh set of security-themed posters and desktop wallpapers to help keep awareness front and centre across your organisation.

Use them in internal comms, digital signage, or around the office!
cyber security posters image
🔍 What's On Our Roadmap?
🪝 Report Email Continued Upgrades: Over the coming month, we plan to continue upgrading our report email capability with a range of automated analysis features designed to make admins’ lives easier. This will include AI-assisted sentiment analysis, attachment sandboxing, URL analysis, and ultimately a spam and phishing confidence score.

🎟️ Catch Us at BSides Canberra 2025: We’re heading to BSides Canberra 2025 as a proud sponsor and we’d love to see you there!

If you’re an Australian-based customer or attending from overseas, make sure to swing by the CanIPhish booth for some quality swag and a good old-fashioned chin wag.

Subject:	RE: [External] Pricing Information for Cove Data Protection
 	 

Hi All,

 

Thanks for the intro Rocco!

 

Below is some key information about our backup solution, Cove, and the pricing.

 

Key points:

 

The pricing is in AUD, GST-exempt
24 x 7 x 365 support
Monthly billing
Committed quantities are on a 12 month term
Autopay of direct debit is required
Data stored in Equinix Sydney
Data is pooled on the MSP level
Device and M365 data pools are separate from each other
 

 

Device-level pricing

 

Option 1:  Standard pricing

 

-                    $71.25 Server (500 GB pooled data)

-                    $14.25 workstation (100GB pooled data)

-                    $7.30 recovery testing – per server per month

-                    $1.45 Backup documents- light workstation backup license that backs up 120 file extensions (no videos or photos)

-                    $0.03 GB overage

 

Option 2 – Data protection plan - $366 base cost (unlocks cheaper pricing, 1000 Backup document licenses at no charge, and 4X data allocation)

 

-                    $52 Physical Server inc. 2TB data pooled

-                    $30 VM server inc. 2TB data pooled

-                    $10 workstations inc. 500GB data pooled

-                    $7.30 recovery testing – per server per month

 

Microsoft 365 pricing

 

 

$2.20 per month per unique user with 1TB of data shared between Exchange, OneDrive, Sharepoint, and Teams (the Teams section in Teams)
The data is pooled on your account. Example: 300 users = 300 TB of pooled data
7 years retention for all data
Exchange and Teams are backed up every 4 hours and Sharepoint and OneDrive is backed up every 6 hours
Data stored in Equinix Sydney
Shared mailboxes and aliases are not charged as a billable user
If a licensed mailbox is converted to a shared one, it will be protected and not billable. However, if a licensed mailbox is converted to a shared mailbox, but data for that user remains in OneDrive or SharePoint, they will still be billable.
For all selected SharePoint sites, Cove will automatically identify which currently licensed users have accessed the protected sites and bill for them accordingly
 

 

Regards,

text:
Subject: Purchase Order & Coordination – JT Environment Migration to Microsoft 365 Cloud

Body:
Dear Tickbox Team,

This is to confirm our agreement and coordination regarding the migration of the JT environment from Tickbox-managed Active Directory to Microsoft 365 Cloud.

Scope of Work:

Transfer of domains (jonestulloch.com, jonestulloch.com.au, jonestulloch.au) with EPP codes and DNS zone files.

Disable Entra Connect synchronization between Tickbox AD and JT Entra.

Migrate mailboxes, user data, and archives into Microsoft 365 tenant.

Transition mail flow from MailGuard to Defender for O365.

Reconfigure Microsoft 365 backups under Jypra’s ownership.

Tickbox Actions:

Provided EPP codes and DNS zone files.

Disabled M365 backups on Tickbox side.

Uploaded user and archive data to shared location.

Jypra Group Actions:

Set up new backups and confirmed transition readiness.

Planned DNS and MX record changes.

Confirmed responsibility for configuring Defender for O365.

Next Steps:

Complete domain transfer and confirm mail flow changes.

Schedule final migration window for mailbox and user cutover.

We appreciate your continued support in ensuring a smooth migration and request confirmation once all DNS and mail security changes are complete.

Best regards,
Naveen Kumar
Senior Security Analyst – Jypra Group

Subject: Purchase Order & DNS Migration – JT Domains to Proofpoint

Body:
Dear Tickbox Team,

This is to confirm Jypra Group’s request to proceed with the DNS and email security migration for JT domains.

Scope of Work:

Update MX and SPF records for jonestulloch.com and jonestulloch.com.au to Proofpoint standard values.

Remove existing Mimecast and MailGuard references from SPF records.

Confirm if jonestulloch.au requires redirection or additional setup.

Review zone files and ensure A records/CNAMEs are correctly configured.

Provide guidance on disabling legacy connectors and enabling inbound/outbound Proofpoint connectors.

Deliverables Expected from Tickbox:

Validation of updated MX/SPF records.

Confirmation of DNS changes for all active JT domains.

Assistance with mail flow cutover to Proofpoint.

Engagement cost estimate for execution support (if Tickbox manages cutover).

Subject:	Re: [External] Urgent Assistance Required: LEAP Desktop Installation Issue [ ref:!00Dw00lMiE.!500Tg0BQHTV:ref ]
 	 

Dear Visweswaran,
 

Thank you for logging a Support Case with LEAP.

We are unable to reach to you at 12:30 pm today.

Please refer to the following steps to resolve the issue

Hold win+ R key together

Go to %localappdata% > Delete "LEAP Office Installation"
Hold win+ R key together
Go back to %appdata% > Delete LEAP Office Installation
 

1. Close Outlook, Word and Excel 

2. Go to ?https://community.leap.com.au/s/downloads and press 'Download LEAP Desktop'? 

(Note: Even if you are on the latest version of LEAP, this acts as a fresh installer which repairs LEAP) 

3. Press 'Download LEAP Desktop' 

4. Accept the install prompts. 

5. Finish install and Log in to LEAP.? 

 

Prabin Thapa

Subject: RE: Case# 1587378 Addressing Vulnerabilities in 11 Systems for Windows Defender [ ref:!00D200m0ae.!500Kd024HS3G:ref ]

Hi Visweswaran,

Apologies for the delay in response.

After reviewing the scanned report below are the findings.

We can see that your systems are vulnerable with QID 92053 due to "MpSigStub.exe Version 1.1.18500.10".
Additionally, it is evident from the screenshot provided that the patches are not indicated as missing within the PM module for this particular vulnerability.

It is possible that the detection of QID 92053 is being triggered by residual traces of MpSigstub.exe found on the system.

To ensure a comprehensive investigation into whether the issue stems from residual files or if the patch is genuinely not appearing as missing, we kindly recommend scheduling a call.

Could you kindly provide us with your time zone and several available time slots for scheduling a call?

Kind Regards,
Muzaffar Bagwan.
Qualys, Inc.
